340: func newHome(ctx context.Context, program string, autoYes bool) *home {
341: 	// Load application config
342: 	appConfig := config.LoadConfig()
343: 
344: 	// Load application state
345: 	appState := config.LoadState()
346: 
347: 	// Initialize storage
348: 	storage, err := session.NewStorage(appState)
349: 	if err != nil {
350: 		fmt.Printf("Failed to initialize storage: %v\n", err)
351: 		os.Exit(1)
352: 	}
353: 
354: 	activeRepoPath, err := filepath.Abs(".")
355: 	if err != nil {
356: 		fmt.Printf("Failed to get current directory: %v\n", err)
357: 		os.Exit(1)
358: 	}
359: 
360: 	project := filepath.Base(activeRepoPath)
361: 	h := &home{
362: 		ctx:                   ctx,
363: 		spinner:               spinner.New(spinner.WithSpinner(spinner.Dot)),
364: 		menu:                  ui.NewMenu(),
365: 		auditPane:             ui.NewAuditPane(),
366: 		statusBar:             ui.NewStatusBar(),
367: 		tabbedWindow:          ui.NewTabbedWindow(ui.NewPreviewPane(), ui.NewDiffPane(), ui.NewInfoPane()),
368: 		storage:               storage,
369: 		appConfig:             appConfig,
370: 		program:               program,
371: 		autoYes:               autoYes,
372: 		state:                 stateDefault,
373: 		appState:              appState,
374: 		activeRepoPath:        activeRepoPath,
375: 		taskStateDir:          filepath.Join(activeRepoPath, "docs", "plans"), // legacy: only for JSON migration
376: 		signalsDir:            filepath.Join(activeRepoPath, ".kasmos", "signals"),
377: 		taskStoreProject:      project,
378: 		instanceFinalizers:    make(map[*session.Instance]func()),
379: 		waveOrchestrators:     make(map[string]*WaveOrchestrator),
380: 		plannerPrompted:       make(map[string]bool),
381: 		coderPushPrompted:     make(map[string]bool),
382: 		pendingReviewFeedback: make(map[string]string),
383: 	}
384: 
385: 	// Always start an embedded task store server. This gives us a local SQLite
386: 	// DB as the single source of truth without requiring a separate process.
387: 	dbPath := taskstore.ResolvedDBPath()
388: 	embSrv, err := taskstore.StartEmbedded(dbPath, 0)
389: 	if err != nil {
390: 		fmt.Printf("Failed to start embedded task store: %v\n", err)
391: 		os.Exit(1)
392: 	}
393: 	h.embeddedServer = embSrv
394: 
395: 	// Default: use the embedded server's URL for the task store client.
396: 	storeURL := embSrv.URL()
397: 	remoteStoreUnreachable := false
398: 
399: 	// If a remote task store is configured, use that URL instead (multi-machine
400: 	// access over tailscale, etc.). The embedded server still runs for audit log
401: 	// DB access via its SQLite store.
402: 	if appConfig.DatabaseURL != "" {
403: 		remoteStore := taskstore.NewHTTPStore(appConfig.DatabaseURL, project)
404: 		if pingErr := remoteStore.Ping(); pingErr != nil {
405: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("remote task store unreachable: %v — falling back to embedded", pingErr)
406: 			remoteStoreUnreachable = true
407: 			// storeURL stays as the embedded server URL
408: 		} else {
409: 			storeURL = appConfig.DatabaseURL
410: 		}
411: 	}
412: 
413: 	h.taskStore = taskstore.NewHTTPStore(storeURL, project)
414: 	h.fsm = taskfsm.New(h.taskStore, project, h.taskStateDir)
415: 
416: 	// One-time migration: import plan-state.json into the DB if it exists.
417: 	// Use the embedded store directly (bypasses HTTP round-trip).
418: 	// Only runs when plan-state.json is present; subsequent boots skip this.
419: 	planStateJSON := filepath.Join(h.taskStateDir, "plan-state.json")
420: 	if _, statErr := os.Stat(planStateJSON); statErr == nil {
421: 		migrated, migrateErr := taskstore.MigrateFromJSON(embSrv.Store(), project, h.taskStateDir)
422: 		if migrateErr != nil {
423: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("plan-state.json migration failed: %v", migrateErr)
424: 		} else {
425: 			log.InfoLog.Printf("migrated %d plans from plan-state.json to DB", migrated)
426: 			if renameErr := os.Rename(planStateJSON, planStateJSON+".migrated"); renameErr != nil {
427: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("failed to rename plan-state.json after migration: %v", renameErr)
428: 			}
429: 		}
430: 	}
431: 
432: 	// Initialize audit logger. Always uses local SQLite regardless of plan
433: 	// store backend — audit events are purely local state.
434: 	if al, err := auditlog.NewSQLiteLogger(dbPath); err != nil {
435: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("audit logger init failed: %v", err)
436: 		h.auditLogger = auditlog.NopLogger()
437: 	} else {
438: 		h.auditLogger = al
439: 	}
440: 
441: 	h.nav = ui.NewNavigationPanel(&h.spinner)
442: 	h.toastManager = overlay.NewToastManager(&h.spinner)
443: 	h.overlays = overlay.NewManager()
444: 
445: 	// Show a warning toast if a remote task store was configured but unreachable
446: 	// (we fell back to the embedded server).
447: 	if remoteStoreUnreachable {
448: 		h.toastManager.Error("remote task store unreachable — using embedded store")
449: 	}
450: 
451: 	permCacheDir := filepath.Join(activeRepoPath, ".kasmos")
452: 	permStore, err := config.NewSQLitePermissionStore(dbPath)
453: 	if err != nil {
454: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("permission store init failed: %v", err)
455: 	} else {
456: 		if migrateErr := config.MigratePermissionCache(permCacheDir, project, permStore); migrateErr != nil {
457: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("permission cache migration failed: %v", migrateErr)
458: 		}
459: 		h.permissionStore = permStore
460: 	}
461: 	h.permissionHandled = make(map[*session.Instance]string)
462: 
463: 	h.tabbedWindow.SetAnimateBanner(appConfig.AnimateBanner)
464: 	h.setFocusSlot(slotNav)
465: 	h.loadTaskState()
466: 
467: 	// Load saved instances
468: 	instances, err := storage.LoadInstances()
469: 	if err != nil {
470: 		fmt.Printf("Failed to load instances: %v\n", err)
471: 		os.Exit(1)
472: 	}
473: 
474: 	h.allInstances = instances
475: 
476: 	// Add instances matching active repo to the nav
477: 	for _, instance := range instances {
478: 		repoPath := instance.GetRepoPath()
479: 		if repoPath == "" || repoPath == h.activeRepoPath {
480: 			h.nav.AddInstance(instance)()
481: 			if autoYes {
482: 				instance.AutoYes = true
483: 			}
484: 		}
485: 	}
486: 
487: 	h.updateSidebarTasks()
488: 
489: 	// Reconstruct in-memory wave orchestrators for plans that were mid-wave
490: 	// when kasmos was last restarted. Must run after loadTaskState and instance load.
491: 	h.rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators()
492: 
493: 	return h
494: }
495: 
496: // activeProject returns the project name derived from the active repo path.
497: // This matches how planstore derives the project name (filepath.Base of the repo path).
498: func (m *home) activeProject() string {
499: 	return filepath.Base(m.activeRepoPath)
500: }
501: 
502: // isUserInOverlay returns true when the user is actively interacting with
503: // any modal overlay. Used to prevent async metadata-tick handlers from
504: // clobbering the active overlay by showing a confirmation dialog.
505: func (m *home) isUserInOverlay() bool {
506: 	switch m.state {
507: 	case stateDefault:
508: 		return false
509: 	}
510: 	return true
511: }
512: 
513: // exitFocusModeForDialog exits focus/interactive mode if active, so that an
514: // incoming dialog (permission prompt, wave completion, planner-finished, etc.)
515: // can be displayed immediately. Focus mode is not a real overlay — it just
516: // forwards keys to the embedded PTY — so it is safe to interrupt.
517: func (m *home) exitFocusModeForDialog() {
518: 	if m.state == stateFocusAgent {
519: 		m.exitFocusMode()
520: 	}
521: }
522: 
523: // updateHandleWindowSizeEvent sets the sizes of the components.
524: // The components will try to render inside their bounds.
525: func (m *home) updateHandleWindowSizeEvent(msg tea.WindowSizeMsg) {
526: 	// Two-column layout: nav + preview
527: 	var navWidth int
528: 	if m.sidebarHidden {
529: 		navWidth = 0
530: 	} else {
531: 		navWidth = msg.Width * 30 / 100
532: 		if navWidth < 25 {
533: 			navWidth = 25
534: 		}
535: 	}
536: 	tabsWidth := msg.Width - navWidth
537: 
538: 	// Keep the keybind rail compact and give the saved rows to the three columns.
539: 	menuHeight := 1
540: 	if msg.Height < 2 {
541: 		menuHeight = 0
542: 	}
543: 	statusBarHeight := 1
544: 	contentHeight := msg.Height - menuHeight - statusBarHeight
545: 	if contentHeight < 1 {
546: 		contentHeight = 1
547: 	}
548: 	// Detect actual terminal resize vs spurious tea.RequestWindowSize side-effects.
549: 	termResized := msg.Width != m.termWidth || msg.Height != m.termHeight
550: 
551: 	m.termWidth = msg.Width
552: 	m.termHeight = msg.Height
553: 	m.toastManager.SetSize(msg.Width, msg.Height)
554: 	if m.statusBar != nil {
555: 		m.statusBar.SetSize(msg.Width)
556: 	}
557: 
558: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetSize(tabsWidth, contentHeight)
559: 
560: 	// Nav panel gets full content height — audit pane is rendered inside its border.
561: 	m.nav.SetSize(navWidth, contentHeight)
562: 	if m.auditPane != nil && m.auditPane.Visible() && navWidth > 0 {
563: 		// Size audit pane for the nav panel's inner content area.
564: 		// border (2) + border padding (2) + item padding (2) = 6
565: 		auditInnerW := navWidth - 6
566: 		// Pass full content height — the nav panel clamps to whatever space
567: 		// remains below the list content (active/plans sections + legend).
568: 		auditH := contentHeight
569: 		if !m.auditBootstrapped {
570: 			m.refreshAuditPane() // load historical events on first render
571: 			m.auditBootstrapped = true
572: 		}
573: 		m.auditPane.SetSize(auditInnerW, auditH)
574: 		m.nav.SetAuditView(m.auditPane.String(), m.auditPane.ContentLines())
575: 	} else {
576: 		m.nav.SetAuditView("", 0)
577: 		if m.auditPane != nil {
578: 			m.auditPane.SetSize(0, 0)
579: 		}
580: 	}
581: 
582: 	// Store for mouse hit-testing
583: 	m.navWidth = navWidth
584: 	m.tabsWidth = tabsWidth
585: 	m.contentHeight = contentHeight
586: 
587: 	if navWidth == 0 && m.focusSlot == slotNav {
588: 		m.setFocusSlot(slotAgent)
589: 	}
590: 
591: 	// Only resize overlays when the terminal dimensions actually changed.
592: 	// Many handlers emit tea.RequestWindowSize as a batched side-effect (e.g.
593: 	// instanceStartedMsg) — those fire with the same dimensions and should
594: 	// not overwrite the overlay's explicit sizing.
595: 	if termResized {
596: 		m.overlays.SetSize(msg.Width, msg.Height)
597: 	}
598: 
599: 	previewWidth, previewHeight := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
600: 	if m.previewTerminal != nil {
601: 		m.previewTerminal.Resize(previewWidth, previewHeight)
602: 	}
603: 	if err := m.nav.SetSessionPreviewSize(previewWidth, previewHeight); err != nil {
604: 		log.ErrorLog.Print(err)
605: 	}
606: 	m.menu.SetSize(msg.Width, menuHeight)
607: }
608: 
609: func (m *home) Init() tea.Cmd {
610: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventSessionStarted, "kasmos started")
611: 
612: 	// Upon starting, we want to start the spinner. Whenever we get a spinner.TickMsg, we
613: 	// update the spinner, which sends a new spinner.TickMsg. I think this lasts forever lol.
614: 	return tea.Batch(
615: 		m.spinner.Tick,
616: 		func() tea.Msg {
617: 			time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
618: 			return previewTickMsg{}
619: 		},
620: 		tickUpdateMetadataCmd,
621: 		m.toastTickCmd(),
622: 		detectClickUpCmd(m.activeRepoPath),
623: 	)
624: }
625: 
626: func (m *home) Update(msg tea.Msg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
627: 	switch msg := msg.(type) {
628: 	case overlay.ToastTickMsg:
629: 		m.toastManager.Tick()
630: 		if m.toastManager.HasActiveToasts() {
631: 			return m, m.toastTickCmd()
632: 		}
633: 		return m, nil
634: 	case prCreatedMsg:
635: 		m.toastManager.Resolve(m.pendingPRToastID, overlay.ToastSuccess, "PR created!")
636: 		m.pendingPRToastID = ""
637: 		m.audit(auditlog.EventPRCreated, fmt.Sprintf("PR created: %s", msg.prTitle),
638: 			auditlog.WithInstance(msg.instanceTitle),
639: 		)
640: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
641: 	case prErrorMsg:
642: 		log.ErrorLog.Printf("%v", msg.err)
643: 		m.toastManager.Resolve(msg.id, overlay.ToastError, msg.err.Error())
644: 		m.pendingPRToastID = ""
645: 		return m, m.toastTickCmd()
646: 	case planRenderedMsg:
647: 		if msg.err != nil {
648: 			return m, m.handleError(msg.err)
649: 		}
650: 		m.cachedPlanFile = msg.planFile
651: 		m.cachedPlanRendered = msg.rendered
652: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
653: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetDocumentContent(msg.rendered)
654: 		return m, nil
655: 	case previewTickMsg:
656: 		// If previewTerminal is active, render from it (zero-latency VT emulator).
657: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil && !m.tabbedWindow.IsDocumentMode() {
658: 			if content, changed := m.previewTerminal.Render(); changed {
659: 				m.tabbedWindow.SetPreviewContent(content)
660: 			}
661: 		} else if m.previewTerminal == nil && !m.tabbedWindow.IsDocumentMode() {
662: 			// No terminal — show appropriate fallback state.
663: 			selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
664: 			if selected != nil && selected.Started() && selected.Status != session.Paused && selected.Status != session.Loading {
665: 				// Instance is running but terminal hasn't attached yet — show connecting indicator.
666: 				m.tabbedWindow.SetConnectingState()
667: 			} else {
668: 				// nil, Loading, or Paused — delegate to UpdatePreview which renders the
669: 				// correct fallback (banner, progress bar, or paused message).
670: 				if err := m.tabbedWindow.UpdatePreview(selected); err != nil {
671: 					log.ErrorLog.Printf("preview update error: %v", err)
672: 				}
673: 			}
674: 		}
675: 		// Advance spring animation every tick (20fps)
676: 		m.tabbedWindow.TickSpring()
677: 		// Banner animation (only when no terminal is active / fallback showing).
678: 		m.previewTickCount++
679: 		if m.previewTickCount%20 == 0 {
680: 			m.tabbedWindow.TickBanner()
681: 		}
682: 		// Use event-driven wakeup when terminal is live, fall back to 50ms poll otherwise.
683: 		term := m.previewTerminal
684: 		return m, func() tea.Msg {
685: 			if term != nil {
686: 				term.WaitForRender(16 * time.Millisecond)
687: 			} else {
688: 				time.Sleep(50 * time.Millisecond)
689: 			}
690: 			return previewTickMsg{}
691: 		}
692: 	case keyupMsg:
693: 		m.menu.ClearKeydown()
694: 		return m, nil
695: 	case clickUpDetectedMsg:
696: 		m.clickUpConfig = &msg.Config
697: 		m.nav.SetClickUpAvailable(true)
698: 		return m, nil
699: 	case clickUpSearchResultMsg:
700: 		if msg.Err != nil {
701: 			// Check if the error is a multiple-workspaces error — show picker instead of failing.
702: 			var mwErr *clickup.MultipleWorkspacesError
703: 			if errors.As(msg.Err, &mwErr) && len(mwErr.WorkspaceIDs) > 0 {
704: 				m.clickUpPendingQuery = msg.Query
705: 				m.state = stateClickUpWorkspacePicker
706: 				// Build picker labels: "name (id)" when names are available, bare id otherwise.
707: 				items := make([]string, len(mwErr.WorkspaceIDs))
708: 				m.clickUpWorkspaceMap = make(map[string]string, len(mwErr.WorkspaceIDs))
709: 				for i, id := range mwErr.WorkspaceIDs {
710: 					if name, ok := mwErr.WorkspaceNames[id]; ok && name != "" {
711: 						label := name + " (" + id + ")"
712: 						items[i] = label
713: 						m.clickUpWorkspaceMap[label] = id
714: 					} else {
715: 						items[i] = id
716: 						m.clickUpWorkspaceMap[id] = id
717: 					}
718: 				}
719: 				m.overlays.Show(overlay.NewPickerOverlay("select clickup workspace", items))
720: 				return m, nil
721: 			}
722: 			m.toastManager.Error("clickup search failed: " + msg.Err.Error())
723: 			m.state = stateDefault
724: 			return m, m.toastTickCmd()
725: 		}
726: 		if len(msg.Results) == 0 {
727: 			m.toastManager.Info("no clickup tasks found")
728: 			m.state = stateDefault
729: 			return m, m.toastTickCmd()
730: 		}
731: 		m.clickUpResults = msg.Results
732: 		items := make([]string, len(msg.Results))
733: 		for i, r := range msg.Results {
734: 			label := r.ID + " · " + r.Name
735: 			if r.Status != "" {
736: 				label += " (" + r.Status + ")"
737: 			}
738: 			if r.ListName != "" {
739: 				label += " — " + r.ListName
740: 			}
741: 			items[i] = label
742: 		}
743: 		m.state = stateClickUpPicker
744: 		m.overlays.Show(overlay.NewPickerOverlay("select clickup task", items))
745: 		return m, nil
746: 	case tickUpdateMetadataMessage:
747: 		// Snapshot the instance list for the goroutine. The slice header is
748: 		// copied but the pointers are shared — CollectMetadata only reads
749: 		// instance fields that don't change between ticks (started, Status,
750: 		// tmuxSession, gitWorktree, Program).
751: 		instances := m.nav.GetInstances()
752: 		snapshots := make([]*session.Instance, len(instances))
753: 		copy(snapshots, instances)
754: 		taskStateDir := m.taskStateDir // snapshot for goroutine
755: 		signalsDir := m.signalsDir     // snapshot for goroutine
756: 		store := m.taskStore           // snapshot for goroutine
757: 		project := m.taskStoreProject  // snapshot for goroutine
758: 
759: 		return m, func() tea.Msg {
760: 			results := make([]instanceMetadata, 0, len(snapshots))
761: 			for _, inst := range snapshots {
762: 				if !inst.Started() || inst.Paused() {
763: 					continue
764: 				}
765: 				md := inst.CollectMetadata()
766: 				results = append(results, instanceMetadata{
767: 					Title:              inst.Title,
768: 					Content:            md.Content,
769: 					ContentCaptured:    md.ContentCaptured,
770: 					Updated:            md.Updated,
771: 					HasPrompt:          md.HasPrompt,
772: 					DiffStats:          md.DiffStats,
773: 					CPUPercent:         md.CPUPercent,
774: 					MemMB:              md.MemMB,
775: 					ResourceUsageValid: md.ResourceUsageValid,
776: 					TmuxAlive:          md.TmuxAlive,
777: 					PermissionPrompt:   md.PermissionPrompt,
778: 				})
779: 			}
780: 
781: 			// Load plan state — moved here from the synchronous Update handler
782: 			// to avoid blocking the event loop every 500ms.
783: 			// Always reads from the store (embedded or remote) — no JSON fallback.
784: 			var ps *taskstate.TaskState
785: 			if taskStateDir != "" {
786: 				var loaded *taskstate.TaskState
787: 				var err error
788: 				loaded, err = taskstate.Load(store, project, taskStateDir)
789: 				if err != nil {
790: 					log.WarningLog.Printf("could not load plan state: %v", err)
791: 				} else {
792: 					ps = loaded
793: 				}
794: 			}
795: 
796: 			// Scan signals from the project-local signals directory (.kasmos/signals/).
797: 			var signals []taskfsm.Signal
798: 			if signalsDir != "" {
799: 				signals = taskfsm.ScanSignals(signalsDir)
800: 			}
801: 
802: 			// Also scan signals from active worktrees — agents write
803: 			// sentinel files relative to their CWD which is the worktree,
804: 			// not the main repo. Worktrees use .kasmos/signals/ as well.
805: 			seen := make(map[string]bool)
806: 			for _, sig := range signals {
807: 				seen[sig.Key()] = true
808: 			}
809: 			for _, inst := range snapshots {
810: 				wt := inst.GetWorktreePath()
811: 				if wt == "" {
812: 					continue
813: 				}
814: 				wtSignalsDir := filepath.Join(wt, ".kasmos", "signals")
815: 				for _, sig := range taskfsm.ScanSignals(wtSignalsDir) {
816: 					if !seen[sig.Key()] {
817: 						seen[sig.Key()] = true
818: 						signals = append(signals, sig)
819: 					}
820: 				}
821: 			}
822: 
823: 			var waveSignals []taskfsm.WaveSignal
824: 			if signalsDir != "" {
825: 				waveSignals = taskfsm.ScanWaveSignals(signalsDir)
826: 			}
827: 
828: 			var elaborationSignals []taskfsm.ElaborationSignal
829: 			if signalsDir != "" {
830: 				elaborationSignals = taskfsm.ScanElaborationSignals(signalsDir)
831: 			}
832: 
833: 			tmuxCount := tmux.CountKasSessions(cmd2.MakeExecutor())
834: 			time.Sleep(200 * time.Millisecond)
835: 			return metadataResultMsg{Results: results, PlanState: ps, Signals: signals, WaveSignals: waveSignals, ElaborationSignals: elaborationSignals, TmuxSessionCount: tmuxCount}
836: 		}
837: 	case metadataResultMsg:
838: 		// Process agent sentinel signals — feed to FSM and consume sentinel files.
839: 		// Done in Update (main goroutine) so FSM writes are never concurrent.
840: 		// Side-effect cmds (reviewer/coder spawns) are collected and batched below.
841: 		var signalCmds []tea.Cmd
842: 		for _, sig := range msg.Signals {
843: 			// Guard: if a wave orchestrator is active for this plan, ignore
844: 			// implement-finished signals. Wave task agents may write this sentinel
845: 			// after completing their individual task, but the wave orchestrator
846: 			// owns the implementing→reviewing transition. Without this guard,
847: 			// the first task to finish prematurely triggers review and pauses
848: 			// sibling tasks, spawning a "applying fixes" coder alongside the
849: 			// reviewer.
850: 			if sig.Event == taskfsm.ImplementFinished {
851: 				if _, hasOrch := m.waveOrchestrators[sig.TaskFile]; hasOrch {
852: 					log.WarningLog.Printf("ignoring implement-finished signal for %q — wave orchestrator active", sig.TaskFile)
853: 					taskfsm.ConsumeSignal(sig)
854: 					continue
855: 				}
856: 			}
857: 
858: 			if err := m.fsm.Transition(sig.TaskFile, sig.Event); err != nil {
859: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("signal %s for %s rejected: %v", sig.Event, sig.TaskFile, err)
860: 				taskfsm.ConsumeSignal(sig)
861: 				continue
862: 			}
863: 			taskfsm.ConsumeSignal(sig)
864: 
865: 			// Side effects: spawn agents in response to successful transitions.
866: 			switch sig.Event {
867: 			case taskfsm.ImplementFinished:
868: 				// Pause the coder that wrote this signal.
869: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
870: 					if inst.TaskFile == sig.TaskFile && inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypeCoder {
871: 						inst.ImplementationComplete = true
872: 						_ = inst.Pause()
873: 						break
874: 					}
875: 				}
876: 				// If this coder was spawned to apply reviewer feedback, post fixer_complete.
877: 				// pendingReviewFeedback is set when ReviewChangesRequested fires and
878: 				// cleared here once the fix-coder delivers its implement-finished signal.
879: 				if _, hasFeedback := m.pendingReviewFeedback[sig.TaskFile]; hasFeedback {
880: 					delete(m.pendingReviewFeedback, sig.TaskFile)
881: 					if cmd := m.postClickUpProgress(sig.TaskFile, "fixer_complete", ""); cmd != nil {
882: 						signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
883: 					}
884: 				}
885: 				if cmd := m.spawnReviewer(sig.TaskFile); cmd != nil {
886: 					signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
887: 				}
888: 			case taskfsm.ReviewApproved:
889: 				planName := taskstate.DisplayName(sig.TaskFile)
890: 				m.audit(auditlog.EventPlanTransition, "reviewing → done (review approved)",
891: 					auditlog.WithPlan(sig.TaskFile))
892: 				m.toastManager.Success(fmt.Sprintf("review approved: %s", planName))
893: 				// Post progress comment to ClickUp.
894: 				if cmd := m.postClickUpProgress(sig.TaskFile, "review_approved", ""); cmd != nil {
895: 					signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
896: 				}
897: 				// Kill the reviewer instance — it's done.
898: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
899: 					if inst.TaskFile == sig.TaskFile && inst.IsReviewer {
900: 						_ = inst.Kill()
901: 						break
902: 					}
903: 				}
904: 			case taskfsm.ReviewChangesRequested:
905: 				feedback := sig.Body
906: 				m.pendingReviewFeedback[sig.TaskFile] = feedback
907: 				// Post progress comment to ClickUp (truncate feedback to avoid huge comments).
908: 				{
909: 					truncated := feedback
910: 					if len(truncated) > 200 {
911: 						truncated = truncated[:200] + "..."
912: 					}
913: 					if cmd := m.postClickUpProgress(sig.TaskFile, "review_changes_requested", truncated); cmd != nil {
914: 						signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
915: 					}
916: 				}
917: 				// Pause the reviewer that wrote this signal.
918: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
919: 					if inst.TaskFile == sig.TaskFile && inst.IsReviewer {
920: 						_ = inst.Pause()
921: 						break
922: 					}
923: 				}
924: 				// Increment the review cycle counter before spawning the fix coder so
925: 				// that spawnCoderWithFeedback reads the updated (post-increment) value.
926: 				if err := m.taskState.IncrementReviewCycle(sig.TaskFile); err != nil {
927: 					log.WarningLog.Printf("could not increment review cycle for %q: %v", sig.TaskFile, err)
928: 				}
929: 				if cmd := m.spawnCoderWithFeedback(sig.TaskFile, feedback); cmd != nil {
930: 					signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
931: 				}
932: 			case taskfsm.PlannerFinished:
933: 				capturedPlanFile := sig.TaskFile
934: 				// Ingest the plan content from the agent's worktree into the DB.
935: 				// Planners run on the main branch, so the plan file is in activeRepoPath.
936: 				m.ingestTaskContent(capturedPlanFile, m.activeRepoPath)
937: 				// Post progress comment to ClickUp (fire-and-forget; nil-safe if no task ID).
938: 				{
939: 					summary := ""
940: 					if m.taskStore != nil {
941: 						if content, err := m.taskStore.GetContent(m.taskStoreProject, capturedPlanFile); err == nil {
942: 							if plan, err := taskparser.Parse(content); err == nil {
943: 								totalTasks := 0
944: 								for _, w := range plan.Waves {
945: 									totalTasks += len(w.Tasks)
946: 								}
947: 								summary = fmt.Sprintf("%d tasks, %d waves", totalTasks, len(plan.Waves))
948: 							}
949: 						}
950: 					}
951: 					if cmd := m.postClickUpProgress(capturedPlanFile, "plan_ready", summary); cmd != nil {
952: 						signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
953: 					}
954: 				}
955: 				if m.plannerPrompted[capturedPlanFile] {
956: 					break
957: 				}
958: 				m.exitFocusModeForDialog()
959: 				if m.isUserInOverlay() {
960: 					// Overlay is active — defer the dialog to the next tick
961: 					// instead of silently dropping it. The sentinel has already
962: 					// been consumed and the FSM transitioned; we must not lose
963: 					// the "show dialog" side effect.
964: 					m.deferredPlannerDialogs = append(m.deferredPlannerDialogs, capturedPlanFile)
965: 					break
966: 				}
967: 				// Focus the planner instance so the user sees its output behind the overlay.
968: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
969: 					if inst.TaskFile == sig.TaskFile && inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypePlanner {
970: 						if cmd := m.focusInstanceForOverlay(inst); cmd != nil {
971: 							signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
972: 						}
973: 						m.pendingPlannerInstanceTitle = inst.Title
974: 						break
975: 					}
976: 				}
977: 				m.pendingPlannerTaskFile = capturedPlanFile
978: 				m.confirmAction(
979: 					fmt.Sprintf("task '%s' is ready. start implementation?", taskstate.DisplayName(capturedPlanFile)),
980: 					func() tea.Msg {
981: 						return plannerCompleteMsg{planFile: capturedPlanFile}
982: 					},
983: 				)
984: 			}
985: 		}
986: 		if len(msg.Signals) > 0 {
987: 			m.loadTaskState() // refresh after signal processing
988: 		}
989: 
990: 		// Retry deferred PlannerFinished dialogs — show the first queued plan
991: 		// whose dialog was skipped because an overlay was active at signal time.
992: 		if len(m.deferredPlannerDialogs) > 0 {
993: 			m.exitFocusModeForDialog()
994: 		}
995: 		if len(m.deferredPlannerDialogs) > 0 && !m.isUserInOverlay() {
996: 			planFile := m.deferredPlannerDialogs[0]
997: 			m.deferredPlannerDialogs = m.deferredPlannerDialogs[1:]
998: 			if !m.plannerPrompted[planFile] {
999: 				for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
1000: 					if inst.TaskFile == planFile && inst.AgentType == session.AgentTypePlanner {
1001: 						if cmd := m.focusInstanceForOverlay(inst); cmd != nil {
1002: 							signalCmds = append(signalCmds, cmd)
1003: 						}
1004: 						m.pendingPlannerInstanceTitle = inst.Title
1005: 						break
1006: 					}
1007: 				}
1008: 				m.pendingPlannerTaskFile = planFile
1009: 				m.confirmAction(
1010: 					fmt.Sprintf("task '%s' is ready. start implementation?", taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)),
1011: 					func() tea.Msg {
1012: 						return plannerCompleteMsg{planFile: planFile}
1013: 					},
1014: 				)
1015: 			}
1016: 		}
1017: 
1018: 		// Process wave signals — trigger implementation for specific waves.
1019: 		for _, ws := range msg.WaveSignals {
1020: 			taskfsm.ConsumeWaveSignal(ws)
1021: 
1022: 			// Check if orchestrator already exists
1023: 			if _, exists := m.waveOrchestrators[ws.TaskFile]; exists {
1024: 				m.toastManager.Error(fmt.Sprintf("wave already running for '%s'", taskstate.DisplayName(ws.TaskFile)))
1025: 				continue
1026: 			}
1027: 
1028: 			// Read and parse the plan from store
1029: 			content, err := m.taskStore.GetContent(m.taskStoreProject, ws.TaskFile)
1030: 			if err != nil {
1031: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("wave signal: could not read plan %s: %v", ws.TaskFile, err)
1032: 				continue
1033: 			}
1034: 			plan, err := taskparser.Parse(content)
1035: 			if err != nil {
1036: 				m.toastManager.Error(fmt.Sprintf("plan '%s' has no wave headers", taskstate.DisplayName(ws.TaskFile)))
1037: 				continue
1038: 			}
1039: 
1040: 			if ws.WaveNumber > len(plan.Waves) {
1041: 				m.toastManager.Error(fmt.Sprintf("plan has %d waves, requested wave %d", len(plan.Waves), ws.WaveNumber))
1042: 				continue
1043: 			}
1044:

34: func mergeTopicStatus(status ui.TopicStatus, inst *session.Instance, started bool) ui.TopicStatus {
35: 	if started && !inst.Paused() && !inst.PromptDetected {
36: 		status.HasRunning = true
37: 	}
38: 	if inst.Notified {
39: 		status.HasNotification = true
40: 	}
41: 	return status
42: }

44: func mergePlanStatus(status ui.TopicStatus, inst *session.Instance, started bool) ui.TopicStatus {
45: 	if inst.TaskFile == "" {
46: 		return status
47: 	}
48: 	if started && !inst.Paused() {
49: 		if inst.IsReviewer {
50: 			status.HasNotification = true
51: 		} else {
52: 			status.HasRunning = true
53: 		}
54: 	}
55: 	if inst.Notified && inst.IsReviewer {
56: 		status.HasNotification = true
57: 	}
58: 	return status
59: }

61: // computeStatusBarData builds the StatusBarData from the current app state.
62: func (m *home) computeStatusBarData() ui.StatusBarData {
63: 	data := ui.StatusBarData{
64: 		FocusMode:        m.state == stateFocusAgent,
65: 		TmuxSessionCount: m.tmuxSessionCount,
66: 		ProjectDir:       filepath.Base(m.activeRepoPath),
67: 	}
68: 
69: 	if m.nav == nil {
70: 		if data.Branch == "" {
71: 			data.Branch = currentBranch(m.activeRepoPath)
72: 		}
73: 		return data
74: 	}
75: 
76: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
77: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
78: 
79: 	switch {
80: 	case planFile != "" && m.taskState != nil:
81: 		entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(planFile)
82: 		if ok {
83: 			data.Branch = entry.Branch
84: 			data.PlanName = taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
85: 			data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
86: 
87: 			if orch, orchOK := m.waveOrchestrators[planFile]; orchOK {
88: 				waveNum := orch.CurrentWaveNumber()
89: 				totalWaves := orch.TotalWaves()
90: 				if waveNum > 0 {
91: 					data.WaveLabel = fmt.Sprintf("wave %d/%d", waveNum, totalWaves)
92: 					tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
93: 					data.TaskGlyphs = make([]ui.TaskGlyph, len(tasks))
94: 					for i, task := range tasks {
95: 						switch orch.taskStates[task.Number] {
96: 						case taskComplete:
97: 							data.TaskGlyphs[i] = ui.TaskGlyphComplete
98: 						case taskFailed:
99: 							data.TaskGlyphs[i] = ui.TaskGlyphFailed
100: 						case taskRunning:
101: 							data.TaskGlyphs[i] = ui.TaskGlyphRunning
102: 						default:
103: 							data.TaskGlyphs[i] = ui.TaskGlyphPending
104: 						}
105: 					}
106: 				}
107: 			}
108: 		}
109: 	case selected != nil && selected.Branch != "":
110: 		data.Branch = selected.Branch
111: 		if selected.TaskFile != "" && m.taskState != nil {
112: 			entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(selected.TaskFile)
113: 			if ok {
114: 				data.PlanName = taskstate.DisplayName(selected.TaskFile)
115: 				data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
116: 			}
117: 		}
118: 	}
119: 
120: 	if data.Branch == "" {
121: 		data.Branch = currentBranch(m.activeRepoPath)
122: 	}
123: 
124: 	return data
125: }

127: // currentBranch returns the name of the currently checked-out branch in repoPath.
128: // Falls back to "main" if the branch cannot be determined (e.g. detached HEAD).
129: func currentBranch(repoPath string) string {
130: 	cmd := exec.Command("git", "branch", "--show-current")
131: 	cmd.Dir = repoPath
132: 	out, err := cmd.Output()
133: 	if err != nil {
134: 		return "main"
135: 	}
136: 	branch := strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
137: 	if branch == "" {
138: 		return "main" // detached HEAD
139: 	}
140: 	return branch
141: }

143: // computePlanStatuses builds per-plan instance status flags (running/notification)
144: // from the current instance list.
145: func (m *home) computePlanStatuses() map[string]ui.TopicStatus {
146: 	planStatuses := make(map[string]ui.TopicStatus)
147: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
148: 		if inst.TaskFile == "" {
149: 			continue
150: 		}
151: 		planSt := planStatuses[inst.TaskFile]
152: 		planStatuses[inst.TaskFile] = mergePlanStatus(planSt, inst, inst.Started())
153: 	}
154: 	return planStatuses
155: }

165: // focusSlot constants for readability.
166: // Order matches tab layout: info (first), agent (second), diff (third).
167: const (
168: 	slotNav   = 0
169: 	slotInfo  = 1
170: 	slotAgent = 2
171: 	slotDiff  = 3
172: 	slotCount = 4
173: )

175: // setFocusSlot updates which pane has focus and syncs visual state.
176: func (m *home) setFocusSlot(slot int) {
177: 	m.focusSlot = slot
178: 	m.nav.SetFocused(slot == slotNav)
179: 	m.menu.SetFocusSlot(slot)
180: 
181: 	// Center pane is focused when any of the 3 center tabs is active.
182: 	centerFocused := slot >= slotInfo && slot <= slotDiff
183: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocused(centerFocused)
184: 
185: 	// When focusing a center tab, switch the visible tab to match and track which tab is focused.
186: 	if centerFocused {
187: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(slot - slotInfo) // slotInfo=1 → InfoTab=0, etc.
188: 	}
189: 	// focusedTab always tracks activeTab so the gradient header is visible
190: 	// regardless of which pane has keyboard focus.
191: }
192: 
193: // nextFocusSlot cycles the visible center tab forward (info → agent → diff → info).
194: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav); only the
195: // displayed tab changes. This is called by Tab and →.
196: func (m *home) nextFocusSlot() {
197: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
198: 	case ui.InfoTab:
199: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
200: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
201: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
202: 	default: // ui.DiffTab — wraps to info
203: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
204: 	}
205: }
206: 
207: // prevFocusSlot cycles the visible center tab backward (info → diff → agent → info).
208: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav).
209: func (m *home) prevFocusSlot() {
210: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
211: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
212: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
213: 	case ui.DiffTab:
214: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
215: 	default: // ui.InfoTab — wraps to diff
216: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
217: 	}
218: }
219: 
220: // enterFocusMode enters focus/insert mode and starts the fast preview ticker.
221: // enterFocusMode reuses the existing previewTerminal if it is already attached to
222: // the selected instance — entering focus just toggles key forwarding to the same
223: // terminal. Only spawns a new terminal if none is attached yet (rare fallback).
224: func (m *home) enterFocusMode() tea.Cmd {
225: 	m.tabbedWindow.ClearDocumentMode()
226: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
227: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused {
228: 		return nil
229: 	}
230: 
231: 	// If previewTerminal is already attached to this instance, just enter focus mode.
232: 	if m.previewTerminal != nil && m.previewTerminalInstance == selected.Title {
233: 		m.state = stateFocusAgent
234: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
235: 		m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
236: 		return nil
237: 	}
238: 
239: 	// No terminal yet (shouldn't normally happen) — spawn one synchronously-ish.
240: 	cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
241: 	if cols < 10 {
242: 		cols = 80
243: 	}
244: 	if rows < 5 {
245: 		rows = 24
246: 	}
247: 	term, err := selected.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
248: 	if err != nil {
249: 		return m.handleError(err)
250: 	}
251: 	m.previewTerminal = term
252: 	m.previewTerminalInstance = selected.Title
253: 	m.state = stateFocusAgent
254: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
255: 	m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
256: 
257: 	return nil
258: }

260: // exitFocusMode resets focus state. previewTerminal stays alive — it continues
261: // rendering in normal preview mode after the user exits focus/insert mode.
262: func (m *home) exitFocusMode() {
263: 	// previewTerminal stays alive — it continues rendering in normal preview mode.
264: 	m.state = stateDefault
265: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(false)
266: 	m.menu.SetFocusMode(false)
267: }

269: // switchToTab changes the visible center tab without stealing focus from the sidebar.
270: // The sidebar (slotNav) always retains keyboard focus.
271: func (m *home) switchToTab(name keys.KeyName) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
272: 	var targetTab int
273: 	switch name {
274: 	case keys.KeyTabAgent:
275: 		targetTab = ui.PreviewTab
276: 	case keys.KeyTabDiff:
277: 		targetTab = ui.DiffTab
278: 	case keys.KeyTabInfo:
279: 		targetTab = ui.InfoTab
280: 	default:
281: 		return m, nil
282: 	}
283: 
284: 	if m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() == targetTab {
285: 		return m, nil
286: 	}
287: 
288: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(targetTab)
289: 	return m, nil
290: }

311: // instanceChanged updates the preview pane, menu, and diff pane based on the selected instance.
312: // It returns a tea.Cmd when an async operation is needed (terminal spawn).
313: func (m *home) instanceChanged() tea.Cmd {
314: 	// selected may be nil
315: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
316: 
317: 	// Clear notification on the previously-viewed instance when the user
318: 	// navigates away. This prevents the item from jumping out of "attention"
319: 	// while the user is still looking at it.
320: 	if m.seenNotified != nil && m.seenNotified != selected {
321: 		m.seenNotified.Notified = false
322: 		m.seenNotified = nil
323: 		m.updateNavPanelStatus()
324: 	}
325: 	if selected != nil && selected.Notified {
326: 		m.seenNotified = selected
327: 	}
328: 
329: 	// Manage preview terminal lifecycle on selection change.
330: 	var spawnCmd tea.Cmd
331: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused || selected.Exited {
332: 		// No valid instance (or dead tmux session) — tear down terminal.
333: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
334: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
335: 		}
336: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
337: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
338: 	} else if selected.Title != m.previewTerminalInstance {
339: 		// Different instance selected — swap terminal.
340: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
341: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
342: 		}
343: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
344: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
345: 
346: 		cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
347: 		if cols < 10 {
348: 			cols = 80
349: 		}
350: 		if rows < 5 {
351: 			rows = 24
352: 		}
353: 		capturedTitle := selected.Title
354: 		capturedInstance := selected
355: 		spawnCmd = func() tea.Msg {
356: 			term, err := capturedInstance.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
357: 			return previewTerminalReadyMsg{term: term, instanceTitle: capturedTitle, err: err}
358: 		}
359: 	}
360: 
361: 	m.tabbedWindow.UpdateDiff(selected)
362: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInstance(selected)
363: 	m.updateInfoPane()
364: 	// Update menu with current instance
365: 	m.menu.SetInstance(selected)
366: 
367: 	// Auto-switch tab based on selection type: plan header → info tab, instance → agent tab.
368: 	// Only auto-switch when the nav panel is focused to avoid hijacking explicit tab selection.
369: 	if m.focusSlot == slotNav {
370: 		if selected != nil {
371: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
372: 		} else if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {
373: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
374: 		}
375: 	}
376: 
377: 	// Collect async commands.
378: 	var cmds []tea.Cmd
379: 	if spawnCmd != nil {
380: 		cmds = append(cmds, spawnCmd)
381: 	}
382: 
383: 	if len(cmds) == 0 {
384: 		return nil
385: 	}
386: 	if len(cmds) == 1 {
387: 		return cmds[0]
388: 	}
389: 	return tea.Batch(cmds...)
390: }

392: // focusInstanceForOverlay selects the given instance in the nav panel and
393: // switches the preview to show its output behind an overlay dialog. The user
394: // can see the agent's terminal behind the overlay to understand context before
395: // responding. Returns a tea.Cmd if an async terminal spawn is needed.
396: func (m *home) focusInstanceForOverlay(inst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {

404: // focusPlanInstanceForOverlay selects the best instance belonging to the given
405: // plan file, preferring running instances. This is used before showing overlay
406: // dialogs about plan lifecycle events (wave completion, etc.) so the user can
407: // see the agent output behind the overlay.
408: func (m *home) focusPlanInstanceForOverlay(planFile string) tea.Cmd {
409: 	var best *session.Instance
410: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
411: 		if inst.TaskFile != planFile {
412: 			continue
413: 		}
414: 		if best == nil {
415: 			best = inst
416: 		}
417: 		// Prefer running instances over ready/paused ones.
418: 		if inst.Status == session.Running && best.Status != session.Running {
419: 			best = inst
420: 		}
421: 	}
422: 	return m.focusInstanceForOverlay(best)
423: }

425: func statusString(s session.Status) string {

429: 	case session.Ready:
430: 		return "ready"
431: 	case session.Loading:
432: 		return "loading"
433: 	case session.Paused:

435: 	default:
436: 		return "unknown"
437: 	}

440: // updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader populates the info tab when a plan header is selected
441: // (no instance). Shows plan metadata and instance summary counts.
442: func (m *home) updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader() {
443: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
444: 	if planFile == "" || m.taskState == nil {
445: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
446: 		return
447: 	}
448: 	entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(planFile)
449: 	if !ok {
450: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
451: 		return
452: 	}
453: 	data := ui.InfoData{
454: 		IsPlanHeaderSelected: true,
455: 		PlanName:             taskstate.DisplayName(planFile),
456: 		PlanStatus:           string(entry.Status),
457: 		PlanTopic:            entry.Topic,
458: 		PlanBranch:           entry.Branch,
459: 	}
460: 	if !entry.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
461: 		data.PlanCreated = entry.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
462: 	}
463: 	// Count instances belonging to this plan.
464: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
465: 		if inst.TaskFile != planFile {
466: 			continue
467: 		}
468: 		data.PlanInstanceCount++
469: 		switch {
470: 		case inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading:
471: 			data.PlanRunningCount++
472: 		case inst.Status == session.Paused:
473: 			data.PlanPausedCount++
474: 		default:
475: 			data.PlanReadyCount++
476: 		}
477: 	}
478: 	// Include wave progress if an orchestrator exists for this plan.
479: 	if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[planFile]; ok {
480: 		data.TotalWaves = orch.TotalWaves()
481: 		data.TotalTasks = orch.TotalTasks()
482: 		tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
483: 		data.WaveTasks = make([]ui.WaveTaskInfo, len(tasks))
484: 		for i, task := range tasks {
485: 			state := "pending"
486: 			if orch.IsTaskComplete(task.Number) {
487: 				state = "complete"
488: 			} else if orch.IsTaskFailed(task.Number) {
489: 				state = "failed"
490: 			} else if orch.IsTaskRunning(task.Number) {
491: 				state = "running"
492: 			}
493: 			data.WaveTasks[i] = ui.WaveTaskInfo{Number: task.Number, State: state}
494: 		}
495: 	}
496: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(data)
497: }

500: func (m *home) updateInfoPane() {
501: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
502: 	if selected == nil {
503: 		// No instance selected — check if a plan header is selected.
504: 		if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {

506: 			return

516: 		Branch:      selected.Branch,
517: 		Path:        selected.Path,

526: 	}

528: 	if selected.TaskFile != "" {
529: 		if m.taskState != nil {
530: 			entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(selected.TaskFile)
531: 			if ok {
532: 				data.HasPlan = true
533: 				data.PlanName = taskstate.DisplayName(selected.TaskFile)
534: 				data.PlanDescription = entry.Description
535: 				data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
536: 				data.PlanTopic = entry.Topic
537: 				data.PlanBranch = entry.Branch
538: 				if !entry.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
539: 					data.PlanCreated = entry.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
540: 				}
541: 			}
542: 		}
543: 
544: 		if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[selected.TaskFile]; ok {
545: 			data.TotalWaves = orch.TotalWaves()
546: 			data.TotalTasks = orch.TotalTasks()
547: 			tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
548: 			data.WaveTasks = make([]ui.WaveTaskInfo, len(tasks))
549: 			for i, task := range tasks {
550: 				state := "pending"
551: 				if orch.IsTaskComplete(task.Number) {
552: 					state = "complete"
553: 				} else if orch.IsTaskFailed(task.Number) {
554: 					state = "failed"
555: 				} else if orch.IsTaskRunning(task.Number) {
556: 					state = "running"
557: 				}
558: 				data.WaveTasks[i] = ui.WaveTaskInfo{Number: task.Number, State: state}
559: 			}
560: 		}
561: 	}
562: 
563: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(data)
564: }

566: // loadTaskState reads plan state from the store for the active repo.
567: // Called on user-triggered events (plan creation, repo switch, etc.). The periodic
568: // metadata tick loads plan state in its goroutine instead.
569: // Silently no-ops if the store is not configured.
570: func (m *home) loadTaskState() {
571: 	if m.taskStateDir == "" || m.taskStore == nil {
572: 		return
573: 	}
574: 	ps, err := taskstate.Load(m.taskStore, m.taskStoreProject, m.taskStateDir)
575: 	if err != nil {
576: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not load plan state: %v", err)
577: 		if m.toastManager != nil {
578: 			m.toastManager.Error("task store error: " + err.Error())
579: 		}
580: 		return
581: 	}
582: 	m.taskState = ps
583: }

585: // updateSidebarTasks pushes the current plans into the sidebar using the three-level tree API.
586: func (m *home) updateSidebarTasks() {
587: 	if m.taskState == nil {
588: 		m.nav.SetTopicsAndPlans(nil, nil, nil)
589: 		return
590: 	}
591: 
592: 	// Build topic displays
593: 	topicInfos := m.taskState.Topics()
594: 	topics := make([]ui.TopicDisplay, 0, len(topicInfos))
595: 	for _, t := range topicInfos {
596: 		plans := m.taskState.TasksByTopic(t.Name)
597: 		planDisplays := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(plans))
598: 		for _, p := range plans {
599: 			if p.Status == taskstate.StatusDone || p.Status == taskstate.StatusCancelled {
600: 				continue // finished/cancelled plans handled separately
601: 			}
602: 			planDisplays = append(planDisplays, ui.PlanDisplay{
603: 				Filename:    p.Filename,
604: 				Status:      string(p.Status),
605: 				Description: p.Description,
606: 				Branch:      p.Branch,
607: 				Topic:       p.Topic,
608: 			})
609: 		}
610: 		if len(planDisplays) > 0 {
611: 			topics = append(topics, ui.TopicDisplay{Name: t.Name, Plans: planDisplays})
612: 		}
613: 	}
614: 
615: 	// Build ungrouped plans
616: 	ungroupedInfos := m.taskState.UngroupedTasks()
617: 	ungrouped := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(ungroupedInfos))
618: 	for _, p := range ungroupedInfos {
619: 		ungrouped = append(ungrouped, ui.PlanDisplay{
620: 			Filename:    p.Filename,
621: 			Status:      string(p.Status),
622: 			Description: p.Description,
623: 			Branch:      p.Branch,
624: 		})
625: 	}
626: 
627: 	// Flatten single-plan topics where topic name matches the plan display name.
628: 	// These don't benefit from a topic header — show the plan directly as ungrouped.
629: 	filtered := topics[:0]
630: 	for _, t := range topics {
631: 		if len(t.Plans) == 1 && t.Name == taskstate.DisplayName(t.Plans[0].Filename) {
632: 			ungrouped = append(ungrouped, t.Plans[0])
633: 		} else {
634: 			filtered = append(filtered, t)
635: 		}
636: 	}
637: 	topics = filtered
638: 
639: 	// Build history
640: 	finishedInfos := m.taskState.Finished()
641: 	history := make([]ui.PlanDisplay, 0, len(finishedInfos))
642: 	for _, p := range finishedInfos {
643: 		history = append(history, ui.PlanDisplay{
644: 			Filename:    p.Filename,
645: 			Status:      string(p.Status),
646: 			Description: p.Description,
647: 			Branch:      p.Branch,
648: 			Topic:       p.Topic,
649: 		})
650: 	}
651: 
652: 	// Set plan statuses before the rebuild so navPlanSortKey uses
653: 	// up-to-date running/notification flags in a single pass.
654: 	m.nav.SetPlanStatuses(m.computePlanStatuses())
655: 
656: 	m.nav.SetTopicsAndPlans(topics, ungrouped, history)
657: 
658: }

660: // checkPlanCompletion scans running coder instances for plans that have been
661: // marked "done" by the agent and, if found, transitions them to reviewer sessions.
662: // Returns a cmd to start the reviewer (may be nil).
663: func (m *home) checkPlanCompletion() tea.Cmd {
664: 	if m.taskState == nil {
665: 		return nil
666: 	}
667: 	// Guard: if a reviewer already exists for a plan, do not spawn another.
668: 	// The async metadata tick can overwrite m.taskState with a stale snapshot
669: 	// that still shows StatusDone after transitionToReview already ran and set
670: 	// StatusReviewing. Without this guard, a second reviewer is spawned.
671: 	reviewerPlans := make(map[string]bool)
672: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
673: 		if inst.IsReviewer && inst.TaskFile != "" {
674: 			reviewerPlans[inst.TaskFile] = true
675: 		}
676: 	}
677: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
678: 		if inst.TaskFile == "" || inst.IsReviewer {
679: 			continue
680: 		}
681: 		if inst.ImplementationComplete {
682: 			continue // already went through review cycle — don't re-trigger
683: 		}
684: 		if reviewerPlans[inst.TaskFile] {
685: 			continue // reviewer already spawned; skip regardless of stale plan state
686: 		}
687: 		if !m.taskState.IsDone(inst.TaskFile) {
688: 			continue
689: 		}
690: 		return m.transitionToReview(inst)
691: 	}
692: 	return nil
693: }
694: 
695: // transitionToReview marks a plan as "reviewing", pauses the coder session,
696: // spawns a reviewer session with the reviewer profile, and returns the start cmd.
697: func (m *home) transitionToReview(coderInst *session.Instance) tea.Cmd {
698: 	// Guard: transition via FSM before next tick re-reads disk, preventing double-spawn.
699: 	planFile := coderInst.TaskFile
700: 	if err := m.fsm.Transition(planFile, taskfsm.ImplementFinished); err != nil {
701: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not set plan %q to reviewing: %v", planFile, err)
702: 		// Mark complete to break the retry loop — checkPlanCompletion fires
703: 		// every tick and would re-attempt this transition indefinitely.
704: 		coderInst.ImplementationComplete = true
705: 		return nil // FSM rejected — plan is not in implementing state, don't spawn reviewer.
706: 	}
707: 
708: 	// Auto-pause the coder instance — its work is done.
709: 	coderInst.ImplementationComplete = true
710: 	if err := coderInst.Pause(); err != nil {
711: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not pause coder instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
712: 	}
713: 
714: 	return m.spawnReviewer(planFile)
715: }
716: 
717: // spawnReviewer creates and starts a reviewer session for the given plan,
718: // using the plan's shared worktree so it reviews the actual implementation branch.
719: // Does NOT perform any FSM transition — the caller is responsible for that.
720: // Solo agent plans are excluded — the user ends those manually.
721: func (m *home) spawnReviewer(planFile string) tea.Cmd {
722: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
723: 		if inst.TaskFile == planFile && inst.SoloAgent {
724: 			return nil
725: 		}
726: 	}
727: 	planName := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
728: 	prompt := scaffold.LoadReviewPrompt(planFile, planName)
729: 
730: 	// Kill any previous reviewer for this plan so the new session gets a fresh
731: 	// tmux session instead of reattaching to a stale/errored one.
732: 	m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, session.AgentTypeReviewer)
733: 
734: 	// Resolve the plan's branch for the shared worktree.
735: 	branch := m.taskBranch(planFile)
736: 	if branch == "" {
737: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not resolve branch for plan %q", planFile)
738: 		return nil
739: 	}
740: 
741: 	cycle, _ := m.taskState.ReviewCycle(planFile)
742: 	reviewerInst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
743: 		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("%s-review-%d", planName, cycle+1),
744: 		Path:        m.activeRepoPath,
745: 		Program:     m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeReviewer),
746: 		TaskFile:    planFile,
747: 		AgentType:   session.AgentTypeReviewer,
748: 		ReviewCycle: cycle + 1,
749: 	})
750: 	if err != nil {
751: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not create reviewer instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
752: 		return nil
753: 	}
754: 	reviewerInst.IsReviewer = true
755: 	reviewerInst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
756: 	reviewerInst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
757: 
758: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(reviewerInst, m.nav.AddInstance(reviewerInst))
759: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(reviewerInst) // sort-order safe, unlike index arithmetic
760: 
761: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned reviewer for %s", planName),
762: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
763: 		auditlog.WithInstance(reviewerInst.Title),
764: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeReviewer),
765: 	)
766: 
767: 	m.toastManager.Success(fmt.Sprintf("implementation complete → review started for %s", planName))
768: 
769: 	shared := gitpkg.NewSharedTaskWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, branch)
770: 	return func() tea.Msg {
771: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
772: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
773: 		}
774: 		if err := m.materializeTaskFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
775: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
776: 		}
777: 		err := reviewerInst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, branch)
778: 		return instanceStartedMsg{instance: reviewerInst, err: err}
779: 	}
780: }

782: func withOpenCodeModelFlag(program, model string) string {
783: 	model = normalizeOpenCodeModelID(model)
784: 	if model == "" {
785: 		return program
786: 	}
787: 
788: 	tokens := strings.Fields(program)
789: 	if len(tokens) == 0 {
790: 		return program
791: 	}
792: 	if filepath.Base(tokens[0]) != "opencode" {
793: 		return program
794: 	}
795: 
796: 	for i, tok := range tokens {
797: 		if tok == "--model" || tok == "-m" {
798: 			if i+1 < len(tokens) {
799: 				return program
800: 			}
801: 			return program
802: 		}
803: 		if strings.HasPrefix(tok, "--model=") {
804: 			return program
805: 		}
806: 	}
807: 
808: 	return program + " --model " + model
809: }
810: 
811: // programForAgent resolves the program command for a given agent type
812: // (e.g. "coder", "planner") using the kasmos config profile. Falls back to
813: // m.program if no profile is configured.
814: //
815: // For typed agents (coder/planner/reviewer), opencode's own --agent flag
816: // handles model selection via its agent config, so we do NOT append --model.
817: // For ad-hoc instances (no agent type), we append --model since there is no
818: // --agent flag to drive model selection.
819: func (m *home) programForAgent(agentType string) string {
820: 	if m.appConfig == nil {
821: 		return m.program
822: 	}
823: 	// Map agent types to config phases or profile names.
824: 	var profile config.AgentProfile
825: 	switch agentType {
826: 	case session.AgentTypeCoder:
827: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("implementing", m.program)
828: 	case session.AgentTypePlanner:
829: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("planning", m.program)
830: 	case session.AgentTypeReviewer:
831: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("quality_review", m.program)
832: 	case session.AgentTypeFixer:
833: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("fixer", m.program)
834: 	case session.AgentTypeElaborator:
835: 		profile = m.appConfig.ResolveProfile("elaborating", m.program)
836: 	default:
837: 		// Ad-hoc — use the "chat" profile if available.
838: 		if p, ok := m.appConfig.Profiles["chat"]; ok && p.Enabled && p.Program != "" {
839: 			profile = p
840: 		} else {
841: 			return m.program
842: 		}
843: 		// Ad-hoc sessions have no --agent flag, so pass --model explicitly.
844: 		prog := profile.BuildCommand()
845: 		return withOpenCodeModelFlag(prog, profile.Model)
846: 	}
847: 	// Typed agents: opencode handles model via --agent <type> + its own config.
848: 	return profile.BuildCommand()
849: }
850: 
851: func normalizeOpenCodeModelID(model string) string {
852: 	model = strings.TrimSpace(model)
853: 	if model == "" || strings.Contains(model, "/") {
854: 		return model
855: 	}
856: 	if strings.HasPrefix(model, "claude-") {
857: 		return "anthropic/" + model
858: 	}
859: 	return model
860: }
861: 
862: // killExistingPlanAgent finds and kills any existing instance for the given plan
863: // and agent type, removing it from both the UI list and persistence list.
864: //
865: // IMPORTANT: Instances are removed from both lists BEFORE killing the tmux
866: // session. This prevents the metadata-tick death-detection from seeing a dead
867: // reviewer in the list and auto-firing ReviewApproved (which would prematurely
868: // mark the plan as done).
869: //
870: // For reviewers, also matches legacy instances that only have IsReviewer set.
871: func (m *home) killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, agentType string) {
872: 	// First pass: identify matching instances by title.
873: 	var titles []string
874: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
875: 		if inst.TaskFile != planFile {
876: 			continue
877: 		}
878: 		match := inst.AgentType == agentType
879: 		// Legacy reviewer instances may only have IsReviewer without AgentType.
880: 		if !match && agentType == session.AgentTypeReviewer && inst.IsReviewer {
881: 			match = true
882: 		}
883: 		if match {
884: 			titles = append(titles, inst.Title)
885: 		}
886: 	}
887: 
888: 	// Second pass: remove from both lists, then kill tmux.
889: 	// Removal first ensures the death-detection tick cannot see the dead instance.
890: 	for _, title := range titles {
891: 		inst := m.nav.RemoveByTitle(title)
892: 		m.removeFromAllInstances(title)
893: 		// Invalidate the preview terminal cache so that a replacement instance
894: 		// with the same title (e.g. a new "applying fixes" coder) gets a fresh
895: 		// terminal instead of showing stale output from the killed session.
896: 		if title == m.previewTerminalInstance {
897: 			if m.previewTerminal != nil {
898: 				m.previewTerminal.Close()
899: 			}
900: 			m.previewTerminal = nil
901: 			m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
902: 		}
903: 		if inst != nil {
904: 			if err := inst.Kill(); err != nil {
905: 				log.WarningLog.Printf("could not kill old %s for %q: %v", agentType, planFile, err)
906: 			}
907: 		}
908: 	}
909: }
910: 
911: // spawnCoderWithFeedback creates and starts a coder session for the given plan,
912: // injecting reviewer feedback into the implementation prompt. Uses the plan's
913: // shared worktree so fixes are applied to the actual implementation branch.
914: // Does NOT perform any FSM transition — the caller is responsible for that.
915: func (m *home) spawnCoderWithFeedback(planFile, feedback string) tea.Cmd {
916: 	planName := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
917: 	prompt := buildImplementPrompt(planFile)
918: 	if feedback != "" {
919: 		prompt += fmt.Sprintf("\n\nReviewer feedback from previous round:\n%s", feedback)
920: 	}
921: 
922: 	// Kill any previous coder for this plan so the new session gets a fresh
923: 	// tmux session instead of reattaching to a stale/errored one.
924: 	m.killExistingPlanAgent(planFile, session.AgentTypeCoder)
925: 
926: 	// Clear the push-prompt dedup flag so this new coder round can trigger
927: 	// the push dialog when it finishes.
928: 	delete(m.coderPushPrompted, planFile)
929: 
930: 	// Resolve the plan's branch for the shared worktree.
931: 	branch := m.taskBranch(planFile)
932: 	if branch == "" {
933: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not resolve branch for plan %q", planFile)
934: 		return nil
935: 	}
936: 
937: 	cycle, _ := m.taskState.ReviewCycle(planFile)
938: 	coderInst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
939: 		Title:       fmt.Sprintf("%s-fix-%d", planName, cycle),
940: 		Path:        m.activeRepoPath,
941: 		Program:     m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeCoder),
942: 		TaskFile:    planFile,
943: 		AgentType:   session.AgentTypeCoder,
944: 		ReviewCycle: cycle,
945: 	})
946: 	if err != nil {
947: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("could not create coder instance for %q: %v", planFile, err)
948: 		return nil
949: 	}
950: 	coderInst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
951: 	coderInst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
952: 
953: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(coderInst, m.nav.AddInstance(coderInst))
954: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(coderInst)
955: 
956: 	detail := ""
957: 	if feedback != "" {
958: 		if len(feedback) > 200 {
959: 			detail = feedback[:200] + "..."
960: 		} else {
961: 			detail = feedback
962: 		}
963: 	}
964: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned coder with reviewer feedback for %s", planName),
965: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
966: 		auditlog.WithInstance(coderInst.Title),
967: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeCoder),
968: 		auditlog.WithDetail(detail),
969: 	)
970: 
971: 	m.toastManager.Info(fmt.Sprintf("review changes requested → re-implementing %s", planName))
972: 
973: 	shared := gitpkg.NewSharedTaskWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, branch)
974: 	return func() tea.Msg {
975: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
976: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
977: 		}
978: 		if err := m.materializeTaskFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
979: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
980: 		}
981: 		err := coderInst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, branch)
982: 		return instanceStartedMsg{instance: coderInst, err: err}
983: 	}
984: }
985: 
986: // spawnElaborator creates and starts an elaborator agent session for the given plan.
987: // The elaborator runs on the main branch (not in a worktree) since it only reads the
988: // codebase and updates the task store — it does not modify files. When it finishes,
989: // it writes an elaborator-finished-<planFile> sentinel that the metadata tick picks up
990: // to advance the orchestrator from WaveStateElaborating to wave 1.
991: func (m *home) spawnElaborator(planFile string) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
992: 	planName := taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)
993: 	prompt := orchestration.BuildElaborationPrompt(planFile)
994: 
995: 	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
996: 		Title:    fmt.Sprintf("%s-elaborator", planName),
997: 		Path:     m.activeRepoPath,
998: 		Program:  m.programForAgent(session.AgentTypeElaborator),
999: 		TaskFile: planFile,
1000: 	})
1001: 	if err != nil {
1002: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1003: 	}
1004: 	inst.AgentType = session.AgentTypeElaborator
1005: 	inst.QueuedPrompt = prompt
1006: 	inst.SetStatus(session.Loading)
1007: 	inst.LoadingTotal = 6
1008: 	inst.LoadingMessage = "elaborating plan..."
1009: 
1010: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(inst, m.nav.AddInstance(inst))
1011: 
1012: 	startCmd := func() tea.Msg {
1013: 		return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: inst.StartOnMainBranch()}
1014: 	}
1015: 
1016: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned elaborator for %s", planName),
1017: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
1018: 		auditlog.WithAgent(session.AgentTypeElaborator))
1019: 
1020: 	m.toastManager.Info(fmt.Sprintf("elaborating plan '%s' before implementation", planName))
1021: 	return m, tea.Batch(tea.RequestWindowSize, startCmd, m.toastTickCmd())
1022: }

1024: func (m *home) materializeTaskFile(planFile, repoPath string) error {
1025: 	if m.taskStore == nil {
1026: 		return nil
1027: 	}
1028: 	content, err := m.taskStore.GetContent(m.taskStoreProject, planFile)
1029: 	if err != nil {
1030: 		return fmt.Errorf("get plan content %s: %w", planFile, err)
1031: 	}
1032: 	plansDir := filepath.Join(repoPath, "docs", "plans")
1033: 	if err := os.MkdirAll(plansDir, 0o755); err != nil {
1034: 		return fmt.Errorf("create plans dir: %w", err)
1035: 	}
1036: 	planPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, planFile)
1037: 	if err := os.WriteFile(planPath, []byte(content), 0o644); err != nil {
1038: 		return fmt.Errorf("write plan file: %w", err)
1039: 	}
1040: 	return nil
1041: }
1042: 
1043: // ingestTaskContent reads the plan markdown file from the given repo path and
1044: // stores its content in the DB via the task store. This bridges the gap between
1045: // agents writing plan files to their worktree and the DB being the source of truth.
1046: // Called when a PlannerFinished signal is processed.
1047: func (m *home) ingestTaskContent(planFile, repoPath string) {
1048: 	if m.taskStore == nil || m.taskState == nil {
1049: 		return
1050: 	}
1051: 	planPath := filepath.Join(repoPath, "docs", "plans", planFile)
1052: 	data, err := os.ReadFile(planPath)
1053: 	if err != nil {
1054: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("ingestTaskContent: cannot read %s: %v", planPath, err)
1055: 		return
1056: 	}
1057: 	// Don't clobber existing DB content with an empty disk file. The
1058: 	// wave-annotation planner writes content directly to the DB via
1059: 	// `kas task update-content`; overwriting with a stale/empty disk
1060: 	// file causes a planning→replanning loop.
1061: 	if strings.TrimSpace(string(data)) == "" {
1062: 		if existing, err := m.taskStore.GetContent(m.taskStoreProject, planFile); err == nil && strings.TrimSpace(existing) != "" {
1063: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("ingestTaskContent: disk file empty but DB has content for %s — skipping overwrite", planFile)
1064: 			return
1065: 		}
1066: 	}

1266: func dedupePlanFilename(plansDir, filename string) string {
1267: 	planPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, filename)
1268: 	if _, err := os.Stat(planPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
1269: 		return filename
1270: 	}
1271: 
1272: 	base := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
1273: 	for i := 2; i < 100; i++ {
1274: 		candidate := fmt.Sprintf("%s-%d.md", base, i)
1275: 		if _, err := os.Stat(filepath.Join(plansDir, candidate)); os.IsNotExist(err) {
1276: 			return candidate
1277: 		}
1278: 	}
1279: 
1280: 	return filename
1281: }

1294: 		if _, ok := ps.Entry(candidate); !ok {
1295: 			return candidate
1296: 		}
1297: 	}
1298: 
1299: 	return filename

1302: // shouldPromptPushAfterCoderExit returns true when a coder session has finished
1303: // and the plan is still in the implementing state. A coder is considered finished
1304: // when its tmux session has exited (tmuxAlive == false) OR when it has returned
1305: // to a prompt after completing its queued work (PromptDetected && !AwaitingWork).
1306: // The latter covers "applying fixes" coders spawned by spawnCoderWithFeedback,
1307: // which finish their work and return to prompt rather than exiting tmux.
1308: func shouldPromptPushAfterCoderExit(entry taskstate.TaskEntry, inst *session.Instance, tmuxAlive bool) bool {
1309: 	if inst == nil {
1310: 		return false
1311: 	}
1312: 	if inst.TaskFile == "" {
1313: 		return false
1314: 	}
1315: 	if inst.AgentType != session.AgentTypeCoder {
1316: 		return false
1317: 	}
1318: 	if inst.SoloAgent {
1319: 		return false
1320: 	}
1321: 	if entry.Status != taskstate.StatusImplementing {
1322: 		return false
1323: 	}
1324: 	// Tmux exited — original single-coder completion path.
1325: 	if !tmuxAlive {
1326: 		return true
1327: 	}
1328: 	// Agent returned to prompt after finishing queued work — covers the
1329: 	// review-feedback coder ("applying fixes") which stays alive in tmux.
1330: 	if inst.PromptDetected && !inst.AwaitingWork {
1331: 		return true
1332: 	}
1333: 	return false
1334: }

1580: 		if entry.Branch == "" {
1581: 			entry.Branch = gitpkg.TaskBranchFromFile(planFile)
1582: 			if err := m.taskState.SetBranch(planFile, entry.Branch); err != nil {
1583: 				return m, m.handleError(fmt.Errorf("failed to assign branch for plan: %w", err))
1584: 			}
1585: 		}
1586: 
1587: 		// Coder and reviewer share the plan's feature branch worktree
1588: 		shared := gitpkg.NewSharedTaskWorktree(m.activeRepoPath, entry.Branch)
1589: 		if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
1590: 			return m, m.handleError(err)
1591: 		}
1592: 		startCmd = func() tea.Msg {
1593: 			if err := m.materializeTaskFile(planFile, shared.GetWorktreePath()); err != nil {
1594: 				return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1595: 			}
1596: 			err := inst.StartInSharedWorktree(shared, entry.Branch)
1597: 			return instanceStartedMsg{instance: inst, err: err}
1598: 		}
1599: 	}
1600: 
1601: 	m.audit(auditlog.EventAgentSpawned, fmt.Sprintf("spawned %s for plan %s", agentType, taskstate.DisplayName(planFile)),
1602: 		auditlog.WithPlan(planFile),
1603: 		auditlog.WithInstance(title),
1604: 		auditlog.WithAgent(agentType),
1605: 	)
1606: 
1607: 	m.addInstanceFinalizer(inst, m.nav.AddInstance(inst))
1608: 	m.nav.SelectInstance(inst)
1609: 	return m, tea.Batch(tea.RequestWindowSize, startCmd)

1612: // getTopicNames returns existing topic names for the picker.
1613: func (m *home) getTopicNames() []string {
1614: 	if m.taskState == nil {
1615: 		return nil
1616: 	}
1617: 	topics := m.taskState.Topics()
1618: 	names := make([]string, len(topics))
1619: 	for i, t := range topics {
1620: 		names[i] = t.Name
1621: 	}
1622: 	return names
1623: }
1624: 
1625: // rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators reconstructs in-memory WaveOrchestrators for plans that
1626: // were mid-wave when kasmos was restarted. Without this, the wave completion monitor and
1627: // the "Mark complete" context menu action are both inoperative after a restart.
1628: //
1629: // For each plan that is StatusImplementing and has task instances (TaskNumber > 0) but
1630: // no orchestrator, we:
1631: //  1. Parse the plan file to get the wave/task structure.
1632: //  2. Fast-forward the orchestrator to the wave the instances are on.
1633: //  3. Mark tasks as complete for instances that are already paused (finished their work).
1634: //
1635: // Tasks that are still running remain in taskRunning state so the metadata tick can
1636: // detect their completion normally (or the user can mark them complete manually).
1637: func (m *home) rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators() {
1638: 	if m.taskState == nil || m.taskStateDir == "" {
1639: 		return
1640: 	}
1641: 
1642: 	// Group task instances by plan file.
1643: 	type taskInst struct {
1644: 		taskNumber int
1645: 		waveNumber int
1646: 		paused     bool
1647: 	}
1648: 	byPlan := make(map[string][]taskInst)
1649: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
1650: 		if inst.TaskNumber == 0 || inst.TaskFile == "" {
1651: 			continue
1652: 		}
1653: 		byPlan[inst.TaskFile] = append(byPlan[inst.TaskFile], taskInst{
1654: 			taskNumber: inst.TaskNumber,
1655: 			waveNumber: inst.WaveNumber,
1656: 			paused:     inst.Paused(),
1657: 		})
1658: 	}
1659: 
1660: 	for planFile, tasks := range byPlan {
1661: 		// Skip if orchestrator already exists.
1662: 		if _, exists := m.waveOrchestrators[planFile]; exists {
1663: 			continue
1664: 		}
1665: 		// Only reconstruct for implementing plans.
1666: 		entry, ok := m.taskState.Entry(planFile)
1667: 		if !ok || entry.Status != taskstate.StatusImplementing {
1668: 			continue
1669: 		}
1670: 
1671: 		// Parse the plan content from store.
1672: 		content, err := m.taskStore.GetContent(m.taskStoreProject, planFile)
1673: 		if err != nil {
1674: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators: cannot read %s: %v", planFile, err)
1675: 			continue
1676: 		}
1677: 		plan, err := taskparser.Parse(content)
1678: 		if err != nil {
1679: 			log.WarningLog.Printf("rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators: cannot parse %s: %v", planFile, err)
1680: 			continue
1681: 		}
1682: 
1683: 		orch := NewWaveOrchestrator(planFile, plan)
1684: 
1685: 		// Determine which wave the instances are on (use the max wave number seen).
1686: 		targetWave := 0
1687: 		for _, t := range tasks {
1688: 			if t.waveNumber > targetWave {
1689: 				targetWave = t.waveNumber
1690: 			}
1691: 		}
1692: 
1693: 		// Fast-forward the orchestrator wave-by-wave up to the target wave.
1694: 		// Waves before the target are considered fully complete.
1695: 		for orch.currentWave < len(plan.Waves) {
1696: 			orch.StartNextWave()
1697: 			if orch.CurrentWaveNumber() == targetWave {
1698: 				break
1699: 			}
1700: 			// Mark all tasks in this earlier wave as complete to advance.
1701: 			for _, t := range plan.Waves[orch.currentWave].Tasks {
1702: 				orch.MarkTaskComplete(t.Number)
1703: 			}
1704: 		}
1705: 
1706: 		// Now apply the actual task states for the current wave.
1707: 		for _, t := range tasks {
1708: 			if t.waveNumber != targetWave {
1709: 				continue
1710: 			}
1711: 			if t.paused {
1712: 				orch.MarkTaskComplete(t.taskNumber)
1713: 			}
1714: 			// Running tasks stay in taskRunning — metadata tick handles them.
1715: 		}
1716: 
1717: 		m.waveOrchestrators[planFile] = orch
1718: 		log.WarningLog.Printf("rebuildOrphanedOrchestrators: restored orchestrator for %s (wave %d, %d tasks)",
1719: 			planFile, targetWave, len(tasks))
1720: 	}
1721: }

1731: 	if err := shared.Setup(); err != nil {
1732: 		return m, m.handleError(err)
1733: 	}

1737: 		prompt := buildTaskPrompt(orch.plan, task, orch.CurrentWaveNumber(), orch.TotalWaves(), len(tasks))
